item.title: During WWII, four brothers from the same Jewish family signed up to fight against the Nazis. Only one of the four brothers survived. His grandson is the current President of Ukraine, Volodymyr Zelensky.

item.title: It is on internet, must be true
item.dc:creator: codrcrew

item.title: Story time with Mr. Lawson
item.description:
This is a tried and true social experiment that students learn the value of common resource. Sorry, for the bad title. Students take from a bowl of gold fish to simulate fishing from a pond. The population of goldfish are repopulated at the end of the round (1 fish per every 3 remaining).
I set up the experiment with some ground rules that were outside the norm. You could buy houses that had refrigeration. They could buy cars that allowed them to go between tables if you wanted to trade fish or commodities. And fishing poles if you wanted to increase your time to harvest (from 3sec to 5sec)
I had one final rule, if you wanted to be creative and expand the rules you could if you had real world examples. Example, "can we sell fish we kept for later use if we have a fridge from the house we bought?". Yes!
Honestly, it happened so fast I just had to sit back and watch. Students were rejecting refugees from other tables who miss managed their resources. Others were extorting the poor by hording the fish in refrigeration and selling them for 100x the market value. One kid jokingly offered a fellow student for fish or cash. A stratification of social standings was created of the haves and the have nots. Soon war broke out over the resources. The numbers were strong with the poor masses but like the game risk I gave more dice to the table that could afford more military equipment. It culminated in a world war that had no winner. All the resources were harvested and sold to pay for each sides war machines. These resources then became unsustainable and all parties starved to death to end the experiment.
The reflection papers that came out of that one experiment were the best I've ever read. Students who never write; wrote multi page essays on the interworking of society and the pitfalls of human nature when faced with situations like starvation, war, and discrimination.
item.dc:creator: Letsleepnotphaseme

item.dc:creator: deafkitten

item.title: I can "feel" this photo. The longer I look at it, the more my smile grows.
item.description:
Such pride, love and respect for her parents.
I took this photo of my daughter Wedge, last week on vacation. She has my sense of humor, only she is a lot quicker than I will ever be.
She is my favorite daughter and is such a beautiful, talented and very smart young woman. She got that from her Mom, my wife of 23 years and counting. (Note - favorite daughter is our little inside joke. She is my only daughter. Also, like Wedge Antilles, she is always has my back at a convention/fundraiser and I can always count on her reliability.)
item.dc:creator: imflukeskywalker

item.title: Now I pronounce you man and man
item.dc:creator: CheePain

item.title: After 5 years, I've proved my parents wrong about video games
item.description:
--- It's not a secret that many parents think video games are bad for their children. The born gamers like us always had to went through hell and high water just to get our hands on the keyboards. And my parents was no exception... "What? A career in gaming? Are you out of your mind?!" - that's what my mom said the first time I brought up wanting to make this passion more than just a hobby.
I had lost count how many family talks and interventions I had to join in after that. And I wish I could say that I had ignored all of that and went after my passion anyway, but I didn't. I listened to them and went to a more normal college... The next 3 years of college was the worst time of my life, I was miserable. Everyday was a constant battle between going to classes or the Internet Cafe. I literally hated every minute at school.
Until one day at class while I was scrolling through the Youtube's feed, I stumbled upon an amazing video from Extra Credits.
It's hard to describe how excited I was watching this, because for the first time, a career in video games didn't seem like a high in the sky idea anymore. It seemed real... seemed possible! And so I made a decision that is going to change my life forever, I'm going to quit college and go after my dream.
I won't bother you with the detail but as usual, life isn't going to just give me what I want. The first 4 years was just me tried and failed, and then tried again, and then failed again...
It wasn't until I met my 3 amazing teammates in 2020 that things started to look brighter...
And finally, 2 years later, I did it... We did it. Our very first game had officially fully release on Steam today, March 24th 2022. Although the feedback from our Early Access players has been amazing so far, I can’t help but get anxious about a million things that could go wrong. The amount of self-doubt is pretty overwhelming right now.
But I think regardless of the result, the game has already given so much. Amazing teammates, tons of learning and skills acquired... But something even more amazing happened along the way, my parents had finally changed their minds about video games and had given me their full supports. They actually played the game yesterday and gotten through the tutorial LOL. I’ll take that. Thank you for reading, And for anyone who's in the same position as I was, I hope this had given you just a little bit of inspiration to take the leap.
item.dc:creator: dustinpham

item.title: Antytila, a band from Ukraine
item.description:
This is Antytila, a famous band from Ukraine. They are pretty good... Their music is on Spotify and I add a video with some of their music. I don't know why but when I upload two videos from my phone, the second always upload without sound. But here are some links with their music. I really like it. They are going to broadcast live on march 29th in a Ed Sheeran concert to aid Ukraine. Sauce: https://twitter.com/antytila_offic/status/1506361929698361346?t=A9cLM5cvGm29cVuJ-U3Gzw&s=19 For me to think about them... Doing their music and now defending their homeland just leaves me without words. I hope this to end soon. Music unites people... Links to their music: https://youtu.be/adzOwkpyWDQ https://youtu.be/licC0ArmHCk https://youtu.be/B9gvM_-WYwo
Sorry for the no sound video.. hopefully you'll listen to them on Spotify or YouTube...
Cat tax (her name is spots)
item.dc:creator: aycaramba

item.title: Norwegian eleven-year old sells "fuck putin" bracelets to aid Ukraine
item.description:
Living far away, in the norwegian city of Kristiansund, but still wanting to do something to help the ukranian people, Helle (10) started making and selling beaded bracelets with supportive messages.
The bracelets have slogans like "Kyiv
Her campaign took of, more people joined, and she has so far sold bracelets for about 8600 dollars (75.000 NOK). All profits go to humanitarian aid in Ukraine. The beads are now mostly sponsored by the local toy stores in her city. Similar initiatives have been started in other norwegian cities.
The bracelets are bought and used by many, here seen on the wrist of a norwegian youth politician in a TV debate on national TV.
During WW2, the jewish population in her home town Kristiansund was deported. Mostly to german concentration camps, were they perished. Helle is the great-great-grandchild of one of the jewish families from this city, who were imprisoned in Norway, and thus survived the war.
Their instagram is called "Perle for fred", which roughly translates to "beading for peace". Not sure if they accept international orders, but feel free to give it a go if you want to. They probably appreciate a follow as well. https://www.instagram.com/perleforfred/ Sources: https://www.tk.no/fuck-putin-hilsen-helle-10/s/5-51-1107464 https://www.tk.no/far-bestillinger-fra-hele-landet-pa-fuck-putin-armband-vi-har-tomt-byen-for-perler/s/5-51-1111965
item.dc:creator: frosketosk

item.title: Send help
item.description: I've googled, I've wikid. I really just need someone to dumb it down for me. Explain it like I was an old white guy who's lived his entire life with privilege so he thought he didn't need to know this stuff. Edit: thanks guys, this all helped a lot. I feel like I actually learned something today. Edit again: a few people have linked this, thank you. John Oliver can usually make stuff very understandable. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EICp1vGlh_U
item.dc:creator: peruvianpocketdoor

item.title: I set up a camera to see what my kitten does when I walk the dog
item.dc:creator: Chelseathedog

item.title: I just don't even know anymore...
item.description: I'm just so lost right now. I divorced last year, ending a toxic relationship of over a decade. I've known this girl for years. She was my only form of support for a long time, and we started dating about 7 months ago. I've been more open and honest with her than anyone in my life, ever. Part of me was always worried it wouldn't really work out because of the long distance aspect (NZ-US), but I still... I don't know. I wanted it to. I'm not a social person, I'm bad at meeting people and opening up. She was, is, I don't know, everything I ever wanted. Last night she told me she's 14 weeks pregnant. So... That's cool. Edit: Definitely not the post I thought would go viral. Sucks that it's apparently such a common thing. Thank you guys for the support and advice. I do have a therapist. I just needed to vent; I'll bring this up with her Friday. There's... minimally more that I chose not to share, but that's the gist of it I guess. I'll survive. It just sucks.
item.dc:creator: IMakeMemesNotComedy

item.title: Watching the Judiciary Committee hearings on Ketanji Brown Jackson's nomination reminds me just how recent all this was.
item.description:
White male students protesting integration pose on Governor's Mansion fence. in Little Rock, Arkansas on September 9, 1958.
May 1956 - The Citizen's Council Newspaper
Two black women watch as integration protest approaches Central High School in Little Rock in August 1959.
Boys paused playing ball to watch white school integration protestors walk south on High Street in Little Rock, Arkansas in August 1959.
White students protesting against school integration stop in front of the Arkansas State Capitol in September 1958
Angry and jeering white high school students confront Ernest Green and Jefferson Thomas in front of cameras on September 25, 1957.
May 1956 - The Citizen's Council Newspaper
White student punches hanging effigy of a black student after a student walkout on October 3, 1957.
March 1959 - The Citizen's Council Newspaper
Alex Wilson being choked and attacked by segregationist at Gov. Orval Faubus's press conference on September 23, 1957.
Elizabeth Eckford heckled and followed by Hazel Bryant on September 4, 1957.
An advertisement for Stonewall Jackson Academy in Florence, South Carolina. The school is an example of a w:segregation academy formed in response to the desegregation of public schools. (1970)
The full caption for this item is as follows: American Red Cross Rest Room for [African American] soldiers and sailors. [African American] sailors in the rest room of the Red Cross headquarters, Branch Number 6, of the New Orleans Chapter. This room has been fitted for the use of the [African American] sailors and soldiers by Louise J. Ross. [ca 1917-1919]
Segregated bathrooms for war workers at the X-10 Graphite Reactor at Oak Ridge, Tennessee that opened in 1943.
June 1956 - The Citizen's Council Newspaper
Little Rock, 1959. Rally at state capitol, protesting the integration of Central High School. Protesters carry US flags and signs reading "Race Mixing is Communism" and "Stop the Race Mixing March of the Anti-Christ".
Sign with American flag "We want white tenants in our white community," directly opposite the Sojourner Truth homes, a new U.S. federal housing project in Detroit, Michigan. A riot among white residents broke out to prevent Negro tenants from moving in. (February 1942)
July 1956 - The Citizen's Council Newspaper
November 1955 - The Citizen's Council Newspaper
item.dc:creator: techfun
